projects.title: Strays (Tracy Crosswhite)
projects.logline: A crime drama for our times — part propulsive investigation, part romance, part character study. An hour-long ongoing series following Tracy Crosswhite, a charismatic but deeply wounded homicide detective, as she relentlessly tackles present day homicides and cold cases in search of the truth for both victims and the loved ones left behind.
projects.source: Book — Robert Dugoni's 12-book Tracy Crosswhite novel series
projects.notes: Set up at Hulu with Sony co-financing. Pilot pitched to Hulu. Outline turned in — waiting for Hulu notes. Season One draws from Books 1 and 3 (My Sister's Grave, In the Clearing). Liz Hannah is strong writer attachment. Katherine Pope and Lauren Stein involved because of Liz Hannah's profile. Ali Roland is covering exec and internal champion.
projects.positioning: Highest-priority project on slate. Strongest Sony relationship activation — full leadership stack engaged. Book IP with 12-book series runway. Hulu buyer attached. Crime procedural with female protagonist, romance element, ongoing series potential.

projects.title: American Proof
projects.logline: A Southern family drama set in the high-stakes world of Kentucky bourbon. When a rising craft distillery pursues backing from a powerful international conglomerate, it triggers a brutal power struggle with a storied old money bourbon dynasty — pitting two families against each other in a battle for control, relevance, and survival.
projects.showrunners: Dawn Prestwich and Nicole Yorkin (attached EP/showrun)
projects.buyer_targets: Netflix, Apple TV+ (primary); AMC, Onyx (active); Passed: Paramount, Max, MGM+, Sony; Checking: Starz
projects.notes: Prestige-forward slate lead. No creator economy attachment. Sarah Acosta currently writing Netflix Animation project — existing Netflix relationship. Prestwich and Yorkin strong track record on prestige drama. Pitching actively — AMC and Netflix meetings have occurred. Sony passed due to competitive book in similar world (FX bought it) — their logic was to stay out of the world after not winning the book.
projects.positioning: Prestige scripted, female-skewing, Southern gothic with commercial ambition. Travels internationally. Strong talent package. Netflix franchise replacement crisis creates opening for well-packaged prestige projects from outside producers. FX's competitive project is small slate/limited platform — could stall, leaving the world open.

projects.title: Unwell
projects.logline: Based on the award-winning fiction podcast, UNWELL is a character-forward psychological horror series that uses genre to explore family, memory, and inherited trauma. Set in a remote Dark Sky community, a disturbing car accident sends Dot Harper into violent visions that force her to reconnect with her estranged daughter — gradually revealing the phenomenon is tied to the town itself, rooted in plausible real-world science.
projects.notes: Based on award-winning fiction podcast by Dani and Tobin. Sony attached. Supervisor search active — Sony has passed on Pam Veasey and Erin Maher & Kay Reindl. Kira Snyder in active conversation. Story-driven not creator-led — podcast is source material and audience infrastructure, not a personality brand. Mother-daughter relationship at center examining generational wounds.
projects.positioning: Audio-to-scripted pipeline. Pre-built audience from podcast. Psychological horror — prestige genre. Handmaid's Tale register. Freeform Sony-aligned target.

projects.title: Fox & Hound
projects.logline: Husband-and-wife PI team Rick Mason and Laura Peers come out of a grief-driven retirement to work a missing persons case that pulls them back into the ultra-wealthy Los Angeles world Laura was born into — and exposes the dark machinery underneath it.
projects.writer: Brian Horiuchi & Rowan Maness (writers); VJ Boyd (Supervising Producer consideration)
projects.notes: Source: Fox & Hound novel by Brian Horiuchi & Rowan Maness. Previously written script available as supplemental read — originally developed at SK Global with Marcy Ross. Script does not lead — too broadcast-shaped. Lead with the book and brief spoken pitch. Supervising producer search underway via Geoff Silverman (Evoke Entertainment) and Jennifer Levine (Untitled Entertainment). VJ Boyd in consideration. Barbara Stepansky flagged as having direct Sony relationship via Outlander credits. Rowan is YA-published.
projects.positioning: Book-first. Crime drama with class dynamics. Husband-wife PI team. Sony Zoom meetings upcoming — lead with book not script.

projects.title: Somnia
projects.logline: When a snuff film surfaces on a revolutionary dream-sharing platform blurring the line between memory and nightmare, a disillusioned tech executive and a morally grey detective are forced into an unlikely partnership — and descend into a dark web conspiracy that threatens the future of the human mind itself.
projects.notes: Pilot and pitch deck complete. Sony passed due to IP conflict — not script quality. Supervisor search active. Top candidates: Veena Sud, Patrick Somerville, Dawn Prestwich & Nicole Yorkin, Simon Kinberg (stretch). Denise has existing relationships with Mickey Fisher, Steven Kane, Dean Georgaris. WME repping the project. Key next step: secure supervisor attachment before taking to buyers.
projects.positioning: High-concept sci-fi thriller. Dream-sharing technology. Tech noir. HBO and Netflix primary targets. Needs supervisor attachment before going out.

projects.title: Norman the Spy
projects.logline: Absent minded. Technologically incompetent. Verbal diarrhea. These are not traits you usually associate with a CIA agent. But when Professor Norman Moskovitz stumbles into a high-stakes hunt for a stolen nuclear weapon, his idiosyncratic skills outweigh them all, propelling this out-of-step history professor into the world of international espionage.
projects.producer: Liz Varner, Elena Blekhter, Kevin Fox, Tristram Shapeero
projects.notes: Spec script stage. Ben Klein writing. Tristram Shapeero attached as director. Priority: medium.
projects.positioning: Action-comedy-thriller. Fish-out-of-water espionage. Character-driven comedy with high-stakes plot. Director attachment is a differentiator at spec stage.

projects.title: Mild Bill (Traffic Cop Project)
projects.logline: A forgettable, milquetoast traffic cop discovers his invisibility is the perfect cover for a life of crime — a modern Breaking Bad-esque descent inspired by a real 1980s story, exploring masculinity, wealth inequality, and the death of the American Dream.
projects.notes: Based on a real 1980s story. Early development. Ben Klein attached as writer.
projects.positioning: Breaking Bad-esque male descent narrative. True story foundation. Sony target. Early stage — needs development before going out.

projects.title: Generational Love
projects.status: on hold — feature adaptation in discussion
projects.logline: When a struggling millennial writer returns home to Michigan and discovers her grandmother is losing her memories to Alzheimer's, she races to document the sweeping, complicated love story of Minnie's past — only to find it becoming an unreliable map for her own messy present.
projects.notes: TV series passed by streamers. Feature adaptation in discussion — keep on slate. Pitch pages revised per Sony notes. Sony passed at renewal — Liz retained despite the pass.
projects.positioning: Dual-timeline prestige romantic drama. Female-skewing. Feature adaptation path being explored. Benched as TV series for now.

projects.title: The Grapevine
projects.status: under review — rights not yet controlled
projects.logline: Taylor Sheridan for women. A middle-aged California history professor converts a family RV into a mobile command center and drives the I-5 corridor to find her missing daughter — building an unlikely coalition of bartenders, sex workers, camp hosts, grieving mothers, and a young journalist along the way.
projects.writer: Alexandra Sokoloff & Craig Robertson (source authors); showrunner TBD
projects.notes: Rights not yet controlled — project under review by Sony. Type B IP — Story Quality. No pre-built audience. Casting argument is the audience acquisition strategy: Kate Winslet as Lou, Charles Melton as Ethan. Outstanding questions: series architecture, rights and deal structure, showrunner target. Sokoloff has screenwriting background.
projects.positioning: Working-class female crime procedural. Road spine — I-5 corridor. 45+ female thriller audience chronically underserved. Sheridan-for-women thesis. Winslet/Melton casting argument solves generational audience coalition.

relationships.nature: First-look deal through Very Varner Inc — renewed as 1+1, effective through approximately May 2027
relationships.notes: Katherine Pope runs TV studio, Lauren Stein runs creative, Ali Roland is covering exec. Renewed April 2026 despite studio cutting most deals and Generational Love pass. Active choice to retain. Pope's bandwidth compressed after absorbing nonfiction division — projects need to arrive more fully formed. Sony shifting from studio-as-development-engine to studio-as-financing-and-distribution. Sony has named YouTube utilization and platform-native content as strategic priorities — language ahead of infrastructure, not yet a concrete brief. Sony support is real but unevenly distributed based on exec taste and bandwidth — Strays has full leadership stack engaged.

relationships.nature: Active opportunity — interviewed for Head of Scripted TV under Matt Shanfield
relationships.notes: Audiochuck expanding into TV/film vertical with Chernin investment. Matt Shanfield hired March 2026. UTA repping. Conversation was introductory — role definition still forming. Non-exclusive windowing structure correct for brand. Scripted adaptations sell project by project, no blanket obligations. Ashley Flowers' curatorial authority is the asset — accessibility is the brand, exclusivity damages it.

Sarah Acosta has existing Netflix relationship via animation project. Netflix concentrating prestige scripted — fewer bets, higher stakes, proven talent. Franchise replacement crisis — lost 7 of top 12 scripted shows within 12 months. American Proof well-positioned as fully packaged prestige project.
Strays set up at Hulu with Sony co-financing. Outline delivered — waiting for notes.

Unwell — supervisor search active, Kira Snyder in conversation
Somnia — supervisor search active before going to buyers
Fox & Hound — Sony Zoom meetings upcoming, supervising producer search via Geoff Silverman and Jennifer Levine

Sony first-look renewal — Katherine Pope/Lauren Stein conversation needed
